description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a predominantly brownish-gray coloration and a rough, granular surface texture. A distinct, pale vein runs diagonally across one side, suggesting natural mineral banding. The stone's surface is uneven, with subtle undulations and a matte finish, indicating exposure to weathering. Its form is somewhat elongated and asymmetrical, tapering at one end and broader at the other. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications. Such stones are commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of landscaping features along sidewalks and pathways.
description: An irregular, brownish-gray stone with a rough, granular surface and a pale mineral vein running diagonally across one side. The stone is dense, asymmetrical, and naturally weathered.
description_view_0: This view shows the broader, rounded side of the stone with a mottled brown surface and subtle texture variations.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the stone's tapering end and the prominent pale vein running diagonally across the surface.
description_view_2: The side view emphasizes the full length of the pale mineral vein and the stone's elongated, asymmetrical profile.
description_view_3: This perspective reveals the stone's rough, granular texture and the transition between its wider and narrower ends.
attribute_location: urban park landscaping or garden path
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: natural mineral veining with a prominent pale band and mottled granular texture
attribute_weathering: rough and matte with visible signs of natural weathering and minor surface smoothing
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: blends naturally with soil, grass, or other stones, often partially embedded or surrounded by vegetation
